caption: Figure 7.4 Backpropagation backward pass #1. We work backward from the end of the function computing the derivatives $\partial\ell_{i}/\partial f_{k}$ and $\partial\ell_{i}/\partial h_{k}$ of the loss with respect to the intermediate quantities. Each derivative is computed from the previous one by multiplying by terms of the form $\partial f_{k}/\partial h_{k}$ or $\partial h_{k}/\partial f_{k-1}$.

text: represent the effects of this chain. Notice that we already computed the second of these derivatives, and the other is the derivative of $\beta_{3} + \omega_{3} \cdot h_{3}$ with respect to $h_{3}$, which is $\omega_{3}$.
